message:
fix(ci): green up post-v0.3.2 Benchmarks, Coverage, Lint, and Tests jobs

Four independent failures on the post-release push, all workflow-level:

1. **Lint / cargo fmt --check**: commit 3749a8d6 ("feat(cli-error)
   already_reported sentinel ...") landed a long `cass_not_initialized(...)`
   call at src/lib.rs:5357 without running rustfmt. Apply rustfmt to that
   single call site.

2. **CI / Rust Tests (ubuntu, macos)**: jobs ran `cargo test --all-features`
   which activates the `strict-path-dep-validation` feature. That feature is
   explicitly opt-in (see build.rs CONTRACTS) and requires sibling repos
   checked out at the pinned revs. Workflows clone siblings with
   `git clone --depth 1 <url> ../<name>`, which grabs HEAD (e.g. asupersync
   e64a95a2), not the pinned rev (2028c111). The feature panicked in
   build.rs validate_strict_git_state with: "HEAD to start with `2028c111`,
   found `e64a95a2...`". Switch every `cargo test/clippy --all-features` in
   ci.yml to `--features "qr encryption backtrace"` (the three non-strict
   features). This keeps `strict-path-dep-validation` opt-in as documented.

3. **Coverage / fsqlite-pager dead-code**: workflow env set
   `RUSTFLAGS: "-D warnings"`. Our Cargo.toml `[patch]` overrides for
   fsqlite crates cause cargo to treat them as workspace members and skip
   the default `--cap-lints allow` applied to registry/git dependencies.
   Upstream fsqlite-pager currently carries a `dead_code` warning on
   `FastPageArray::clear` at rev aed640a2; with `-D warnings` this became a
   hard error. Drop the env-level `RUSTFLAGS: "-D warnings"`. Lint
   enforcement still happens in ci.yml's Lint job; coverage measures
   coverage, not lint.

4. **Benchmarks / No space left on device**: the ubuntu-latest runner
   exhausted disk during `Downloaded asupersync v0.2.9`. The bench job
   compiles the full crate graph including many sibling path-dep
   workspaces. Add a "Free disk space on runner" step that removes the
   preinstalled dotnet/Android/GHC/CodeQL caches, reclaiming ~15-20 GiB
   before checkout.

No user-facing behavior change; no release needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -30,6 +30,24 @@ jobs:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 45
     steps:
+      - name: Free disk space on runner
+        # The benchmark job compiles the full crate graph (including many
+        # sibling path-dep workspaces) and previously hit `No space left on
+        # device` during dependency download. Reclaim ~15-20 GiB by removing
+        # preinstalled toolchains we don't use (Android, dotnet, Haskell,
+        # GHC, large caches).
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -eux
+          sudo rm -rf /usr/share/dotnet || true
+          sudo rm -rf /usr/local/lib/android || true
+          sudo rm -rf /opt/ghc || true
+          sudo rm -rf /opt/hostedtoolcache/CodeQL || true
+          sudo rm -rf /usr/local/share/boost || true
+          sudo rm -rf "$AGENT_TOOLSDIRECTORY" || true
+          sudo docker image prune --all --force || true
+          df -h
+
       - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
         with:
           fetch-depth: 0  # For comparing with main

@@ -178,7 +178,11 @@ jobs:
         run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
 
       - name: Run clippy
-        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
+        # Explicit feature list excludes `strict-path-dep-validation`, which is
+        # intentionally opt-in and requires specific sibling-repo git revisions
+        # (see build.rs CONTRACTS). `--all-features` would activate it and make
+        # the build fail because CI clones siblings at HEAD, not pinned revs.
+        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --features "qr encryption backtrace" -- -D warnings
 
   # Rust unit tests
   test-rust:
@@ -207,7 +211,9 @@ jobs:
         uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@ad397744b0d591a723ab90405b7247fac0e6b8db # v2
 
       - name: Run tests
-        run: cargo test --all-features --verbose -- --nocapture
+        # Explicit feature list excludes `strict-path-dep-validation` — see the
+        # Lint job for rationale.
+        run: cargo test --features "qr encryption backtrace" --verbose -- --nocapture
         env:
           RUST_LOG: debug
 
@@ -227,7 +233,9 @@ jobs:
           for t in "${tests[@]}"; do
             args+=(--test "$t")
           done
-          E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --all-features --verbose "${args[@]}" -- --nocapture
+          # Explicit feature list excludes `strict-path-dep-validation` — see
+          # the Lint job for rationale.
+          E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --features "qr encryption backtrace" --verbose "${args[@]}" -- --nocapture
 
       - name: Validate E2E JSONL logs
         if: always()

@@ -19,7 +19,12 @@ on:
 
 env:
   CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
-  RUSTFLAGS: "-D warnings"
+  # NOTE: `-D warnings` intentionally NOT set here. The coverage job compiles
+  # sibling path-dep crates via `[patch]` in Cargo.toml, which makes them
+  # behave like workspace members and bypass cargo's default `--cap-lints
+  # allow` for dependencies. Upstream warnings (e.g. fsqlite-pager
+  # dead_code) must not fail the coverage run — lint enforcement lives in
+  # the Lint job (ci.yml).
   # Phased coverage threshold - update per docs/COVERAGE_POLICY.md schedule
   COVERAGE_THRESHOLD: 60
